steps.title: Use the corresponding angles
steps.content: The triangles are congruent, so corresponding angles are congruent. Since $A$ corresponds to $D$ and $B$ and $E$ are both right angles, the remaining angles $C$ and $F$ must correspond.

steps.title: Use angle sum to find angle C
steps.content:
In any triangle, the sum of angles is $180^{\circ}$. In triangle $ABC$:
$$27^{\circ} + 90^{\circ} + \text{angle } C = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\text{angle } C = 63^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Find angle F using congruence
steps.content: Since the triangles are congruent with A corresponding to D and B corresponding to E, the remaining vertices must correspond: C and F. Congruent triangles have equal corresponding angles, so angle F = angle C = $63^{\circ}$. That matches Choice B — $63^\circ$.
